_meta.schema_note: Leader uses governance_maturity instead of dependency, and peer_benchmark instead of personal metrics. Readiness bands match teacher/support for consistency across staff roles.
_meta.description: Copy tiers for school leader benchmark results. Audience: headteachers, deputy heads, SLT, governors. Language: governance, accountability, legal duty.

emerging.label: Emerging
emerging.consequence: Your school has significant AI risk exposure. Without leadership action, staff are likely using AI tools without consistent safeguards, oversight, or policy guidance in place.
emerging.urgent_action: Complete your AI data protection review with your DPO before the next staff AI tool use.

developing.label: Developing
developing.consequence: Your school has foundational AI awareness but gaps remain in policy, staff practice and oversight. Without focused action, risk exposure will grow as AI use increases.
developing.urgent_action: Appoint an AI lead if you have not already and set a policy review date this term.

established.label: Established
established.consequence: Your school has solid AI governance foundations in place. Focus now on the areas where practice is still inconsistent or policy is not fully embedded.

strong.label: Strong
strong.consequence: Your school demonstrates strong AI readiness across most domains. You are ahead of most schools — priority now is closing remaining gaps and maintaining standards as AI tools evolve.

leading.label: Leading
leading.consequence: Your school is operating at the highest level of AI readiness. Your governance, safeguarding and oversight practices represent a model other schools can learn from.

critical.label: Not yet in place
critical.consequence: No consistent policy, AI lead or oversight process exists. Staff and pupils have no clear standard for AI use and the school has no documented position on AI governance.

developing.label: Gaps in policy & practice
developing.consequence: Initial awareness exists but staff practice and oversight are inconsistent across the school.

partial.label: Partially embedded
partial.consequence: Policy exists but is not consistently followed or understood by all staff. Some departments are ahead of others.

mostly.label: Mostly embedded
mostly.consequence: Good governance structures are in place. Focus on closing gaps in practice and ensuring annual review cycles are met.

full.label: Fully embedded
full.consequence: Governance is strong, regularly reviewed and well understood across the school. A model for other schools to follow.

critical.consequence: Your school has very high AI risk across multiple domains. Immediate leadership action is required before risk materialises into a safeguarding or compliance incident.

high.consequence: Significant risk across key domains. Most schools at Emerging stage sit in this range. Focused action on your top two domains will have the biggest impact.

moderate.consequence: Some domains are well managed but others carry meaningful risk. Targeted improvement in your weakest areas will move your overall score significantly.

low.consequence: Your school is managing AI risk well across most areas. Remaining exposure is concentrated in one or two domains — review these specifically.

minimal.consequence: AI risk is well controlled across all domains. Maintain your current standards and schedule an annual review to stay ahead as tools evolve.

critical.consequence: Your school has not yet assessed whether AI tools could produce unfair or discriminatory outputs affecting pupils. This is a safeguarding and Public Sector Equality Duty risk.

low.consequence: AI tools can reflect and reinforce bias against pupils based on race, gender, SEND status and other protected characteristics. Without a policy-level response this risk is unmanaged.

developing.consequence: Some awareness of AI bias exists at school level but it is not yet embedded in policy or practice. Build this into your AI governance framework and next safeguarding review.

strong.consequence: Your school actively assesses whether AI outputs could be unfair or discriminatory before they affect pupils. This is above the standard of most schools at your phase.

headline.emerging: Your school needs visible AI governance action before informal use becomes embedded risk.
headline.developing: Foundations are forming, but policy, staff practice and oversight need sharper ownership.
headline.established: Your school has solid AI governance foundations. Focus on embedding practice consistently.
headline.strong: Your school is in a strong position. Close the remaining gaps and evidence the review cycle.
headline.leading: Your school can model responsible AI readiness and share governance practice with others.

data_protection_zero.title: Complete your AI data protection review with your DPO
data_protection_zero.body: Your data protection score is 0%. Pupil data may be entering AI tools without a DPIA or approved tool list in place — this is your highest-priority legal and safeguarding risk right now.

safeguarding_zero.title: Update your safeguarding policy to cover AI-enabled harm
safeguarding_zero.body: Your safeguarding score is 0%. DSL and pastoral teams may not be equipped to recognise or respond to deepfake or AI-enabled incidents — this needs to be addressed before your next KCSIE review.

both_critical.title: Meet with your DPO and DSL this week
both_critical.body: Both data protection and safeguarding are at critical level. These are your two highest-priority legal and pastoral risks and should be addressed together as a matter of urgency.

governance_critical.title: Appoint an AI lead and begin drafting your school AI policy
governance_critical.body: Your governance score is critically low. Without an AI lead or policy in place, there is no consistent standard for how staff and pupils use AI across the school.

human_oversight_critical.title: Introduce a whole-school AI verification standard
human_oversight_critical.body: Staff are using AI regularly but there is no consistent check before outputs reach pupils. Some departments are likely passing AI-generated content directly into lessons without review.

assessment_critical.title: Conduct a JCQ-aligned assessment review before the next exam season
assessment_critical.body: Your assessment controls may not yet reflect current JCQ and Ofqual expectations. Inconsistent rules across departments put your school at risk of malpractice incidents.

all_strong.title: Share your results with governors and schedule your next annual review
all_strong.body: Your school is performing well across all domains. The next step is embedding AI governance into your annual review cycle and reporting to governors.

data_protection_awareness.label: Data protection awareness
data_protection_awareness.description: Whether staff understand what pupil data can enter AI tools and whether a DPIA and approved tool list are in place.

critical.summary: Staff may not consistently understand what pupil data can enter AI tools. Without a DPIA and approved tool list, your school has no documented basis for lawful processing under UK GDPR.
impact: Staff choosing AI tools individually without data review
Pupil names, SEND data or safeguarding notes potentially entering third-party AI
No audit trail if an ICO complaint or subject access request is raised
actions: Complete the AI data protection checklist with your DPO
Publish clear staff guidance on what data can enter AI tools
Review your approved tool list against ICO expectations

high.summary: Data protection awareness is inconsistent across the school. Some staff understand the risks but practice varies by department.
actions: Audit which AI tools are currently in use across the school
Clarify data handling rules with your DPO
Update your approved tool guidance and share with all staff

moderate.summary: Most staff understand data protection basics but gaps remain in documentation or approved tool coverage.
actions: Complete or refresh your DPIA for pupil-facing AI tools
Confirm your approved tool list is current and accessible to all staff

safeguarding.label: Safeguarding
safeguarding.description: Whether safeguarding procedures cover AI-specific harms — deepfakes, impersonation and synthetic content — in line with KCSIE.

critical.summary: Safeguarding procedures may not yet cover AI-specific harms. KCSIE expects DSLs to understand deepfakes, AI-enabled impersonation and synthetic content as part of online safety provision.
impact: Online safety procedures in place but AI-specific harms not covered
DSL and pastoral teams may not recognise deepfake or AI impersonation incidents
No AI scenario training means staff may not know how to respond or report
actions: Update your safeguarding policy to include AI-enabled harm
Train your DSL and pastoral teams on deepfake risks
Add AI scenarios to annual safeguarding training

high.summary: Safeguarding covers some AI risks but not consistently. DSL awareness may be limited to general online safety rather than AI-specific scenarios.
actions: Review your safeguarding policy against current KCSIE AI guidance
Brief your DSL on deepfake and impersonation risks
Add at least one AI scenario to your next safeguarding training session

moderate.summary: Core AI safeguarding risks are covered. Focus on keeping pace as new AI-enabled harms emerge.
actions: Schedule an annual review of AI content in your safeguarding policy
Confirm all pastoral staff have received AI scenario training

human_oversight.label: Human oversight
human_oversight.description: Whether staff are consistently verifying AI outputs before they reach pupils or appear in school communications.

critical.summary: Staff are using AI regularly but verification is inconsistent across departments. Some departments are likely passing AI output to pupils without checking it first.
impact: Inconsistent staff practice across departments
Over-trust in AI outputs without verification
Uncertainty about appropriate classroom use
actions: Introduce a whole-school AI verification standard
Embed the Verify Before You Trust framework in CPD
Require sign-off on AI-generated materials before classroom use

high.summary: Most staff verify AI outputs but practice is not yet consistent across all departments or year groups.
actions: Audit verification practice across departments
Share the Verify Before You Trust framework with remaining staff
Add AI verification to your next CPD cycle

moderate.summary: Staff are generally verifying AI outputs. Focus on maintaining standards and supporting newer staff.
actions: Include AI verification in induction for new staff
Review practice annually as part of your AI governance cycle

assessment_integrity.label: Assessment controls
assessment_integrity.description: Whether assessment controls reflect current JCQ and Ofqual expectations for AI use in assessed work.

critical.summary: Assessment controls may not yet reflect current JCQ and Ofqual expectations. AI rules are likely inconsistent across departments.
impact: Inconsistent AI rules communicated across year groups and subjects
Malpractice risk if pupils are unclear on what is permitted
Potential JCQ compliance gap
actions: Conduct a JCQ-aligned assessment review across departments
Update malpractice and exam supervision procedures
Communicate AI rules clearly and consistently to all pupils

high.summary: Some departments have updated assessment controls but practice is not consistent school-wide.
actions: Identify which departments have not yet reviewed their AI assessment guidance
Share a standard template for communicating AI rules to pupils
Confirm malpractice procedures are up to date

moderate.summary: Assessment controls are broadly aligned with JCQ expectations. Maintain awareness of guidance updates.
actions: Schedule an annual JCQ alignment check
Confirm all new staff are briefed on current AI assessment rules

governance_consistency.label: Governance consistency
governance_consistency.description: Whether AI governance is documented, owned by a named lead, and consistently applied across the school.

critical.summary: No consistent policy, AI lead or oversight process exists. Staff and pupils have no clear standard for AI use and the school has no documented governance position.
impact: No named AI lead or ownership of AI risk
Staff making individual decisions without a policy framework
No governor visibility of AI risk or school AI use
actions: Appoint an AI lead from your SLT
Begin drafting a school AI policy using the DfE framework
Set a date for your first formal AI governance review

high.summary: Some governance structures are in place but they are not consistently applied. Policy may exist on paper without reaching staff practice.
actions: Review whether your current AI policy is understood and followed by staff
Assign ownership of AI governance to a named SLT member
Set a calendar date for annual AI policy review

moderate.summary: Governance foundations are in place. Focus on embedding practice and ensuring all staff know what the policy requires.
actions: Communicate your AI policy clearly to all staff
Include AI governance in your next staff briefing cycle
Confirm governor awareness of your current AI position

bias_awareness.label: Bias & fairness oversight
bias_awareness.description: Whether the school has assessed whether AI tools could produce unfair or discriminatory outputs affecting pupils.

critical.summary: Your school has not yet assessed whether AI tools could produce biased or discriminatory outputs. This is a safeguarding concern and a Public Sector Equality Duty risk.
impact: AI outputs potentially disadvantaging pupils by race, gender, SEND status or other protected characteristics
No policy-level response to AI bias risk
Potential PSED breach if AI is used in pupil assessment or grouping decisions
actions: Add AI bias to your next safeguarding policy review
Brief staff that AI outputs can reflect discriminatory patterns
Introduce a bias check as part of your AI verification standard

high.summary: Some awareness of AI bias exists at leadership level but it is not embedded in policy or staff practice.
actions: Include bias awareness in your next staff AI CPD session
Add AI bias to your approved tool assessment criteria
Raise AI fairness in your next governor AI briefing

moderate.summary: Bias awareness is developing. Build it into your governance framework and annual review cycle.
actions: Reference PSED compliance in your school AI policy
Include bias checking in your Verify Before You Trust framework
Monitor for AI outputs that could disadvantage particular pupil groups

ai_literacy.label: AI literacy
ai_literacy.description: Staff confidence and understanding of how AI tools work, where they fail and what their outputs can be trusted for.

critical.summary: Staff AI literacy across the school is low. Without a shared understanding of how AI works and fails, verification and oversight practices will not improve on their own.
impact: Staff unable to recognise AI hallucination or confident errors
No shared vocabulary for discussing AI risk at department level
Difficult to enforce policy when staff do not understand the underlying tools
actions: Schedule a whole-school AI literacy session this term
Share a plain-English guide to AI limitations with all staff
Make AI literacy a standing item in your next CPD calendar

high.summary: Basic AI awareness exists but gaps remain in understanding how tools fail, hallucinate or reflect bias.
actions: Focus CPD on common AI failure modes — hallucination, bias, confident errors
Identify staff with stronger AI literacy to support colleagues
Include AI literacy outcomes in your school improvement plan

moderate.summary: Staff AI literacy is reasonable. Deepen understanding of bias and failure modes across all departments.
actions: Commission targeted AI literacy CPD for departments with lower scores
Build AI literacy assessment into your annual staff review cycle

zero.intro: You have completed the leader audit. When your staff, students and parents complete theirs, you will unlock aggregated school data — including teacher AI dependency, student verification skills and parent awareness gaps.
zero.cta: Roll out to your school community

started.intro: You are getting started. Keep sharing — your whole-school data unlocks at 20 responses and will show you where the real gaps are across all groups.

nearly.intro: Nearly there. A few more responses and your aggregated school data will be ready — including a full risk heatmap across all roles.

unlocked.intro: Your whole-school benchmark is now live. Use this data in your governor report and annual AI review.

strengths.data_protection_76: Clear data protection governance — staff understand what pupil data can and cannot enter AI tools.
strengths.safeguarding_76: Safeguarding procedures cover AI-specific harms including deepfakes and AI-enabled impersonation.
strengths.human_oversight_76: Strong whole-school verification standards — AI output is reviewed before reaching pupils.
strengths.assessment_integrity_76: Assessment controls are aligned with JCQ expectations and consistently communicated to pupils.
strengths.governance_consistency_76: AI governance is documented, owned and embedded in school practice.
strengths.ai_literacy_76: Staff AI literacy is strong — a foundation for effective verification and oversight.
strengths.bias_awareness_76: Your school actively assesses whether AI outputs could be unfair or discriminatory to pupils.

emerging.title: Governance review & readiness consultation
emerging.description: A structured review with an AI readiness specialist — covering where your risks sit, what to prioritise and what your school needs to move from Emerging to Established.

developing.title: School AI policy & governance build
developing.description: You have awareness but need structured governance. This session will produce a draft school AI policy, an approved tool list and a 90-day action plan for your SLT.

established.title: Whole-school CPD programme
established.description: Your governance is in place — now build staff confidence and consistency. A targeted CPD programme based on your highest-risk domains across the whole school.
includes: Verify Before You Trust staff training

strong.title: AI Champion programme
strong.description: Your school is ready to build internal expertise. The AI Champion Programme develops teacher leads who can sustain AI readiness from within.

leading.title: National benchmark & case study
leading.description: Your school is operating at the highest level. Contribute to the UK School AI Benchmark 2027 and share your approach with the wider sector.
